title: elevate: Stand up the AAIF monthly-drop pipeline; first drop = azigler/skills (already matches the vercel-labs/skills standard)
description:
Six slice-opportunities independently pointed at AAIF; the cross-cut is they're not six ideas but one standing monthly obligation plus a ready backlog. Set up the recurring 'one compendium asset -> one public-agents/AGENTS.md drop' cadence and ship the strongest first: azigler/skills (paragon set already matches an emerging cross-agent standard, 50+ agents installable via `npx skills add`; zero hands over the `repair` structured-diagnostics pattern so `br` ships agent-ergonomic; the linters/hooks/bead-discipline pipeline IS a publishable opinionated harness). Queue subsequent drops in leverage order (tmux-lexicon fleet-glyphs -> geometry agent recipe -> MUD-as-ORS env). Andrew has CLAUDE.md but no AGENTS.md - the harness AGENTS.md is itself a drop.

**Leverage:** high
**Explorations:** zero, loop-engineering, ship-the-system, spacedock, agentcad, articraft, blightmud, autonovel, openreward, fledgling, marketing-skills, tolaria
**Why this rank:** Converts a time-sensitive recurring obligation into a forcing function with near-zero new build; biggest cross-cluster signal in the sweep.

Source: /elevate compendium sweep 2026-06-29 (refs/elevate/sweep-2026-06-29.md). Fresh max-effort agents, cross-cluster synthesis.

---

title: skills: gateway learning+hardening bundle (/gateway-audit,-harden,-cost,-watch,-trace) — plan from real egress data, build, try, harden
notes:
SCOPE (the part Zig is most excited about): skills that LEARN + HARDEN from the gateway egress data. Bundle:
- /gateway-audit — what did my fleet do: per-agent tools, denials, model/token usage. (FOUNDATION, build first.)
- /gateway-harden — derive least-privilege CEL policy from observed behavior (the improve loop). HIGHEST leverage.
- /gateway-cost — per-agent/model spend + budget/routing advice.
- /gateway-watch — pulse-style anomaly loop (403 spikes, out-of-scope tool, cost spike) -> beads + notify. HIGH leverage.
- /gateway-trace — explain an agent session trace.

PLAN from the gateway REAL egress-data capabilities (DEP: explore-if4y capability map) — not assumed data. Then build a first cut, try on live gateway data, harden. Each closes the upward seam (DESIGN §3): output lands in the harness human layer (beads/pulse/tmux-lexicon).

DEP: explore-if4y. ACCEPTANCE: >=1 skill built + tried on real data + hardened (rec /gateway-audit then /gateway-harden); SKILL.md (Agent Skills format) in aaif/.claude/skills/. Could itself be an AAIF public-agents contribution.
REFS: ~/explore/agentgateway/DESIGN.md §5, FINDINGS.md. FEEDS: aaif-715 (the article substance).

title: submission: first AAIF piece — perspective article 'The harness, the loop, and the gateway are converging' (Zig voice, andrewzigler.com)
notes:
THE FIRST AAIF SUBMISSION. A cutting-edge PERSPECTIVE/thesis piece (beginner how-to TABLED; its draft ~/explore/agentgateway/tutorial-draft.md is superseded).
THESIS: the harness (orchestrator), the loop (Anthropic loop-engineering + its four costs), and the gateway (agentgateway) are converging. The gateway is the enforcement + o11y layer atop the loop: harness CONVENTIONS become gateway POLICY (budgets->cost caps, capability scopes->CEL tool-authz, egress rules->allowlists); gateway TELEMETRY becomes the harness human layer. See DESIGN §2 (four-loop-costs mapping) + §3 (the two seams).
ANTI-SLOP: grounded in the REAL build — o11y/gov plane on pico (explore-if4y) + the skills (aaif-5ad) + verified findings (frontier o11y incl. a Max subscription; per-agent identity). Earned perspective, not vapor.
VOICE: /zig-voice long-form (specific-moment open, NO negative parallelism, em-dash <=~1/200w, banned-vocab check). Vendor-neutral; one-link rule.
FLOW: compile -> /aaif-review self-review (predict type blog_post 15; classify honestly) -> Zig MANUAL check -> publish to andrewzigler.com via the Zettelkasten vault (NOT ~/andrewzigler3/in/camp, a build-clone) -> (Zig explicit go-ahead) submit.
HARD GATE: never publish or submit without Zig. DEPS: explore-if4y, aaif-5ad. REFS: all of ~/explore/agentgateway/. RELATED: aaif-ig1 (tailscale tutorial idea, folded as supporting), aaif-...20/.9 (wg-observability fit).

title: epic: AAIF Ambassador — top-ambassador via steady monthly contributions
description: Master epic for Andrew's inaugural (2026) AAIF Ambassador work. Goal: be a TOP ambassador by maximizing points x quality x real developer usefulness, sustained monthly. Floor = 1 public project-tied contribution/month; aim = exceed it. Four projects: MCP, goose, AGENTS.md, agentgateway. Playbook: refs/program/strategy-top-ambassador.md. Rules/points: refs/program/ambassador-program.md. Prime directive: no agent slop — every contribution passes a human-verify gate. Children: setup, cadence guard, submissions (the unit of work), idea backlog, working-group participation, research.

title: system: AAIF submission + score-checking pipeline (conformance skill + scorecard optimizer)
description:
GOAL: build a hardened conformance skill + a submission/score-checking pipeline so our submissions pass AAIF's AUTOMATED reviewer at the highest legitimate score, first time. Possibly stage candidates from Zig's LinearB/Dev Interrupted work where it ties to an AAIF project (vendor-neutral).

INITIAL DETAILS ALREADY GATHERED (2026-07-01) → see .local/research/aaif-review-system.md (PRIVATE — has the scorecard schema, review mechanics, 5 typed examples, the rubric/optimization levers). Scorecard-file list saved at .local/research/scorecard-list.txt (36 scorecards in aaif/ambassadors under scorecards/2026/{06,07}/).

KEY FINDING: scoring is TYPE-BASELINE-DRIVEN (blog=15 even for a deep case study; tutorial=20; talk=30; small merged PR=5; total_points==base_points in all observed). The reviewer is goose (posts via @angiejones), leaves a hidden 'aaif-ambassador-review' metadata comment, and opens a scorecard PR writing scorecards/YYYY/MM/issue-NNN-<handle>-<slug>.json. It verifies type/project/publish-date/authorship/dev-value + timing eligibility (artifact must be dated AFTER start 2026-06-23).

NEXT SESSION (dedicated): (1) tabulate ALL 36 scorecards to confirm type→points + find any bonus/human-review/rejection cases + how project-contribution scope maps 5→50 [shell python3/awk/jq-in-loop hit PATH issues; fetch each JSON via gh api contents --jq .content | base64 -d, or dispatch a subagent]; (2) grab the exact [Submission] issue-template; (3) build the hardened skill in aaif/.claude/skills/ (schema + rubric + pre-submit checklist + issue-body drafter, STOPPING at the submit gate); (4) design the pipeline (candidate→classify-type→shape-for-verifiability→draft→Zig-review→submit→track scorecard); (5) LinearB/DI candidate-staging lane.

GUARDRAILS (hard rules, see CLAUDE.md): never post any AAIF issue/PR/comment without alerting Zig + review; never write about other participants in committed content (that analysis stays in .local).

title: skills: gateway bundle extensions — /gateway-cost, /gateway-watch, /gateway-trace (build on the shipped audit+harden core)
submission: hands-on gist walkthrough — 'Build the o11y & governance plane' (real screenshots, Step 0-5+convergence)
description:
The active anchor deliverable (Zig decision 2026-07-03: GIST NOW, decide the andrewzigler.com perspective piece later; relates to aaif-715).

WHAT: a hands-on, screenshot-driven gist walking the REAL steps we did standing up the agentgateway o11y+governance plane — grounded in the live deploy (findings.md), not placeholders. Spine = recovered article.md (Step 0->4 + convergence) + a Step 5 reality-check (the honest gotchas = anti-slop credibility).

WORKING FILES (submissions/2026-07-harness-loop-gateway/, gitignored):
- walkthrough-outline.md  <- the v1 outline; per-step LEARNING MESSAGE (the iterable unit) + screenshot slot
- findings.md             <- raw live-demo numbers (526 calls, cache_read 35.9M, 403/429, p95 52s, 4 gotchas)
- article.md              <- recovered Step 0->4 tutorial-with-thesis draft (placeholder infra)

SCREENSHOT RIG: stand up agentgateway (linux-amd64) locally on THIS box, bind /ui to localhost, seed the exact scenarios, drive Playwright (~/andrewzigler3/node_modules/playwright; npx playwright install chromium) to capture Logs/Analytics/Playground; render DB/skill outputs as styled HTML cards (/impeccable+/dataviz) and screenshot those. NO tailnet (localhost/LAN only). Zig REVIEWS the shots; capture is autonomous.

PIPELINE: iterate learning messages w/ Zig -> build screenshot rig -> capture -> assemble gist draft -> /zig-voice pass -> /aaif-review (predict type: tutorial ~20pts) -> Zig MANUAL sign-off -> publish gist -> (Zig explicit go-ahead) submit.

HARD GATES: never publish/submit any AAIF surface without Zig's explicit go-ahead; no other-participant content in tracked files (landscape stays in .local). 

OPEN Qs (in walkthrough-outline.md): which learning messages land; how many screenshots; real-vs-seeded numbers on screen; single vs multi-file gist; Step 5 standalone vs gotchas folded in.
notes:
DRAFT COMPLETE + SCRUTINIZED + ON GIST (v4). honk (curated MCP nucleus) built + verified on the self-hosted gateway: tools/list = exactly 6; move_file denied ("Unknown tool"); goose end-to-end read/write/fetch all 200. Tutorial "Every goose needs its plane" — dual-track (human why + agent how), zig-voice, honest Step-5 reality-check. Gist (SECRET review draft): https://gist.github.com/azigler/11ee0236e263b31d9d2237070941e961 ; public publish = andrewzigler.com flat-md (bead .34).

SCREENSHOT PASS DONE (2026-07-04): (1) redacted the Servers shot — regenerated the endpoint cells with ~-generalized paths (removed absolute home paths), kept @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem + mcp-server-fetch. (2) captured richer Tool Playground shots from the gateway's live UI via Playwright: 02-tools = dropdown showing the 6 honk tools + Result "6 tools discovered / HTTP 200"; 03-tool-call = live fetch(example.com) -> HTTP 200 + content. (3) CDN-hosted all under aaif/honk-tutorial/ (00-overview, 01-servers-redacted, 02-tools, 03-tool-call); deleted the pre-redaction Servers object (edge un-purgeable w/o token, R2 copy gone). (4) embedded 4 images in the gist (Servers->redacted, +tools in Step 1, +call in Step 2); cleaned the stale gist description.

REMAINING: Zig's v4 line-notes (apply + re-check voice/facts); for public publish — strip the internal randomize-provenance HTML comment + finalize title; re-enable goose's developer extension (disabled for the demo); then Zig's explicit go-ahead to publish + submit (hard rule).

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Image hosting is now LIVE (explore-q9qb done): Cloudflare R2 behind https://cdn.zig.computer, /cdn skill global (up/get/ls/rm/purge, content-addressed immutable URLs). The gist's screenshots (currently on metis, not embedded because gist rejects binary) can now get permanent embeddable CDN URLs via:  cdn.sh --key aaif/goose/<name>.png <file>. See .34 for the hosting+embed step before the andrewzigler.com flat-markdown publish.

title: setup: /aaif-radar skill — weekly AAIF submissions-landscape scanner (trends, over-rotation, opportunities, grading calibration)
description:
A project-local skill (.claude/skills/aaif-radar/) that scans the aaif/ambassadors submissions repo weekly (read-only, via gh api) and answers: what's being submitted + scored, where's the bloat/over-rotation, what lanes are under-served (our opportunities/inspiration), and is Angie's grading still matching our documented model.

TWO OUTPUTS (hard-rule split — CRITICAL):
1. FULL report -> .local/radar/YYYY-Www.md (PRIVATE, gitignored): may reference specific participants/submissions (landscape awareness). Includes a running ledger so week-over-week trends work (delta since last run).
2. DISTILLED note -> a CLOSED 'note' bead in aaif's .beads (COMMITTED): participant-FREE. Only the generalized model — type/project distribution trends, scoring calibration (new types seen, rejections, drift vs our TYPE->BASE_POINTS map), and opportunity lanes framed around developer value. NO named peers, NO ranking, NO 'better than X'. This preserves what we learn without violating the no-gossip rule.

ALSO: if notable (over-rotation, a fresh opportunity, a scoring-model drift), file a P1 human: bead + push notification. Keep /aaif-review's model calibrated: flag any observed type->points that diverges from aaif-review-system.md.

MODES: interactive (Zig types /aaif-radar) may surface via AskUserQuestion; autonomous (timer-fired) NEVER blocks — writes report + note bead, notifies if notable, ends. Read-only: never posts to any AAIF surface.

Grounding: .local/research/aaif-review-system.md (the scoring model + schema), CLAUDE.md hard rules, the existing /aaif-review skill (participant-free model). Pairs with R2 (the systemd timer).

title: setup: confirm hashtag #AAIFAmbassador vs #AAAmbassador (human: ambassadors@aaif.io)
description: The social sharing guide is internally inconsistent: body says use #AAIFAmbassador on every post; tagging table says AAIF monitors #AAAmbassador. Confirm which they actually track. Until then use #AAIFAmbassador primary + #AAAmbassador secondary. Ref: refs/program/brand-and-social.md.

title: setup: pulse-aaif-radar systemd timer (Sat 15:00 PT) — fires /aaif-radar weekly
description:
systemd --user timer + service following the harness pulse convention (pulse-<name>.{timer,service} -> pulse-inject.sh). Fires /aaif-radar into an aaif-anchored tmux window weekly.

Timer: OnCalendar=Sat *-*-* 15:00:00 America/Los_Angeles, Persistent=true.
Service: Type=oneshot, ExecStart=%h/dotfiles/agents/scheduler/pulse-inject.sh --dir /home/ubuntu/aaif --session work --window aaif --cmd "/aaif-radar".

Direct-inject pattern (like pulse-daily-digest / pulse-elevate) — no full /pulse routing-table opt-in needed; the skill resolves because the session anchors at ~/aaif. Keep DISABLED until /aaif-radar (R1) is built + tested once. Depends on R1.

title: spec: goose 'nucleus' toolset — curated lean baseline (read/write/fetch + essentials) behind agentgateway (replaces the omni shim)
description:
FINDING (2026-07-04): goose 1.41 is effectively TOOL-LESS. Its config had only a test 'omni' shim (agentgateway 1.3.1 on the local mesh) exposing just everything_echo + thinking_sequentialthinking; the built-in developer extension was NOT enabled. Shim now DISABLED (goose config enabled:false). So goose can't read/write/fetch/run anything real — a clean blank slate.

GOAL: spec a lean, DELIBERATE 'nucleus' of real tools (the baseline every practitioner wants: read, write, fetch + a few essentials), federated + curated behind agentgateway (virtual MCP + CEL mcpAuthorization), replacing the shim. This becomes the tutorial's centerpiece + screenshots ('scope your own lean goose toolkit').

Dispatched a research/design agent to produce the spec: name candidates (on-theme), exact tool selection w/ rationale, VERIFIED agentgateway 1.3.1 config (virtual-MCP targets + CEL curation), goose wiring, local-model serving, and a verification plan. Then /check -> /test -> /impl -> wire up. NAME is Zig's final call (see AskUserQuestion). Two-tier: keep infra generic in any public-bound material.
notes: SPEC COMPLETE (agent, verified vs agentgateway 1.3.1 source + goose 1.41 + live probes). NAME = 'honk' (Zig 2026-07-04). Nucleus = 6 tools / 2 servers: filesystem read_text_file/write_file/edit_file/list_directory/search_files + fetch. Showcase exclusion = move_file (invisible+uncallable+logged, the V3 negative test). Verified agentgateway config (virtual MCP targets + CEL mcpAuthorization; gotcha: mcp.tool.name is UN-prefixed). goose wiring = streamable_http extension 'honk' replacing the disabled shim. Local model = Qwen3-8B-GGUF Q4_K_M via goose local-models OR ollama qwen3:8b (context trap: raise OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH to 32k). V1-V4 verification plan + 4 honest verify-during-build unknowns. Full spec: submissions/2026-07-harness-loop-gateway/nucleus-spec.md. NEXT: /check -> /test -> /impl (build honk for real), then runner captures screenshots.

title: setup: tutorial-build runner — buildout -> run-through -> screenshots -> assembly (reusable system)
description:
A repeatable SYSTEM for producing the tutorial (and the team-version #3): 
PHASE 1 buildout — reproducibly stand up agentgateway (local) + goose + a local Qwen (8B via goose local-models) + the nucleus toolset (curated MCP federation + CEL). 
PHASE 2 run-through — drive goose through the tutorial's real steps to generate genuine traffic/logs for screenshots. 
PHASE 3 screenshots — Playwright against /ui (Logs/Analytics/Playground) + rendered skill-output cards, per the outline's exact screenshot specs. 
PHASE 4 assembly — assemble the dual-track tutorial (human=why/impact prose in zig-voice; agent=prompts+skills+references) + the images into the final gist. 
Depends on the nucleus spec (sibling bead).
notes: HONK BUILT + VERIFIED on pico (2026-07-04). Applied to pico's live gateway config (~/.config/agentgateway/config.yaml, backed up to .bak-pre-honk-*): swapped shim targets everything/thinking -> filesystem (workspace /Users/pico/honk-workspace) + fetch (uvx mcp-server-fetch); goose allowlist -> the 6 honk tools. Restarted launchd com.zig.agentgateway. VERIFIED over HTTP with goose key: V1 PASS (tools/list = exactly filesystem_read_text_file, write_file, edit_file, list_directory, search_files + fetch_fetch; shim tools gone); V3 PASS (filesystem_move_file -> 'Unknown tool', invisible+uncallable). goose (this box) wired: extension renamed omni->honk, enabled, points at pico:15001/mcp. Model kept qwen3-coder:30b (revisit qwen3:8b for the tutorial's 'accessible local model' narrative at run-through). NEXT: run-through (goose exercises read/write/fetch -> real log traffic) -> screenshots (Playwright vs pico:15000/ui, framed) -> assemble tutorial.

title: setup: tutorial art direction — retrospective + /randomize composition plan + new-aesthetics research (images TABLED 2026-07-04)
description:
IMAGES TABLED (Zig, 2026-07-04) to focus on honk + screenshots + tutorial. This bead holds the art-direction retrospective + the plan for when we return.

WHAT WE TRIED (submissions/2026-07-harness-loop-gateway/images/):
- Round 1 (8 styles of a goose piloting a biplane): retrofuturist-pop, aviation-deco, blueprint, flat-vector, risograph, watercolor, pop-comic, isometric-3D. Zig liked #1 (retrofuturist), #4 (flat-vector), #6 (watercolor), #8 (isometric).
- Round 2: refined those 4 + 4 blends. Zig initially liked the painterly diorama (b6x8) but ultimately rejected the diorama direction.
- Header pass (AAIF-recolor of the diorama): rejected -- colors bolder but not matching.
- Pivot: multi-ref (batch1 #1 + the AAIF ambassador logo), recolor to black+orange, pair the ambassador CHARACTER with the goose as a 'Where in the World is Carmen Sandiego' vigilante duo, strict NO-TEXT control. char = too extreme / black-on-black / illegible. char2 = legible full-palette (black+orange dominant + periwinkle/cream), better but still rejected.

WHAT WE LEARNED / DIDN'T LIKE (constraints for next time):
1. Black+orange must be DOMINANT but NOT the only colors -- need a full, legible palette (periwinkle, cream, sky tones, real value range). Pure black-on-black = illegible.
2. MASCOT FIDELITY (critical): the AAIF ambassador is an ALL-BLACK, FACELESS silhouette. Its ONLY facial feature is the round ORANGE monocle-lens; it also has a black trench coat, a black fedora with an orange hatband, and a small antenna with an orange tip. The generations wrongly gave it a face/eyes -- MUST prompt: faceless solid-black silhouette, no eyes, no visible face, monocle is the only feature.
3. Compositions got 'messy' / cluttered (busy space elements: planets, satellites, stars). Need cleaner, calmer compositions.
4. Text control WORKED when explicit ('absolutely no text/letters/numbers/symbols') -- keep that; add curated text only in post if wanted.

THE PLAN (when we return to images):
A. /randomize for NEW COMPOSITIONS -- don't reuse the single goose-in-biplane-in-sky comp Zig gave. Run /randomize over composition axes to force diversity, e.g.: SCENE (cockpit interior / on the tarmac / map-room war-table / rooftop stakeout / hangar / control tower / open sky), CAMERA (close two-shot / wide establishing / over-the-shoulder / low heroic / top-down), SUBJECT ARRANGEMENT (duo side-by-side / goose leading, agent following / back-to-back / conspiratorial huddle), MOOD (heist-caper / triumphant / sneaky-nefarious / cozy-partners), MEDIUM (flat vector / screenprint / gouache / cel / paper-cut), ERA-NOD (noir / 60s-spy / retrofuturist). Record the seed+provenance per /randomize discipline.
B. RESEARCH NEW AESTHETICS beyond the 8 tried -- candidates to explore: mid-century spy-thriller title-card (Saul Bass), Carmen-Sandiego cel-animation, noir woodcut, cut-paper/collage, gouache travel-poster, ligne-claire (Tintin), screenprint gig-poster, matte editorial vector. Pull Zig's cfp painterly refs (~/cfp/talk-ai-council/refs/msb-aesthetic) + the zig-voice mood-board + AAIF brand as image-to-image anchors.
C. KEEP: the character+goose DUO concept, the caper/vigilante energy, AAIF black+orange DOMINANT with legible support palette, faceless-black-mascot fidelity, strict text control.

Current best-legible asset: submissions/.../images/char2/a1-mascot.png (on metis ~/Downloads). Not final.
notes: CORRECTION to mascot fidelity (Zig 2026-07-04): the AAIF ambassador is NOT a faceless black silhouette (my earlier note was WRONG). It's a friendly cartoon secret-agent: a LIGHT/WHITE face with black cartoon line-features -- two eyes (one round with a highlight, one behind the round ORANGE dotted monocle-lens) and a big open smile -- under a black fedora, wearing a black trench coat with light lapels + buttons, an antenna with a ball tip on the hat, carrying an 'AAIF' briefcase, one white-gloved hand raised. KEY RULE: keep the FACE LIGHT with black features. Do NOT fill the character solid black -- that's the error that looked wrong. Formula: black outlines + white face + black features + orange monocle + black hat/coat. Ref: refs/brand/aaif-ambassador-white.png (clearest read of the line-art).

title: publish-blocker: tutorial ships as flat markdown on andrewzigler.com -> images need CDN-hosted public URLs (not local files)
description:
The 'Every goose needs its plane' tutorial (aaif-ambassador-program-18o.28) will publish as a FLAT MARKDOWN file on andrewzigler.com. Zig will NOT put images in his public site folder (doesn't scale). So EVERY image (the /ui screenshots + the generated art) needs a stable PUBLIC URL to embed in the markdown.

Interim (gist review): gists CAN hold image files, so the gist draft is fine. But the FINAL andrewzigler.com publish is blocked until we have image hosting.

TRACKED BY (research + the fix): explore-q9qb in ~/explore -- 'cheap/easy public image CDN + upload->URL skill' (also kills the scp image-review loop). Its results land back here.

DON'T FORGET before publish: all embedded images must be CDN URLs, not relative paths. (submissions/.../screenshots/ + images/ are gitignored local working copies only.)
notes: UPDATE 2026-07-04: /cdn skill is BUILT (~/.claude/skills/cdn, Cloudflare R2 -> cdn.zig.computer, up/get/ls/rm/purge, content-addressed immutable urls). BLOCKED on one-time setup only Zig can do: enable R2 in Cloudflare (needs payment method even for free tier), create an R2 S3 API token, set the cdn.zig.computer custom domain, put R2_ACCOUNT_ID/R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID/R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY/R2_BUCKET/CDN_BASE_URL in ~/.secrets. Once done: upload the honk-tutorial screenshots -> embed CDN urls in TUTORIAL.md (gist renders inline + publishable to flat markdown). Setup runbook in the skill's SKILL.md.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
RESOLVED — CDN dependency (explore-q9qb) is DONE + LIVE (2026-07-04).

Cloudflare R2 set up with Zig: bucket zig-cdn, custom domain https://cdn.zig.computer serving from R2, verified end-to-end (upload -> public URL -> HTTP 200, correct content-type). Backend decision was R2 + custom domain (branded), chosen over GitHub/jsDelivr.

The /cdn skill graduated to the global paragon set: ~/.claude/skills/cdn (canonical in ~/dotfiles/agents/skills/cdn). Full lifecycle up/get/ls/rm/purge; content-addressed => immutable public URLs; free-tier limits documented (10 GB storage / 1M Class-A / 10M Class-B / egress free); creds in ~/.secrets. A cache-poisoning bug (post-upload HEAD negative-caches a 404 for ~4h) was found on the first live run and fixed (verification is authoritative via R2, public probes are cache-busted).

To host the tutorial images:
  cdn.sh --key aaif/goose/<name>.png <file>   ->  https://cdn.zig.computer/aaif/goose/<name>.png

This bead's BLOCKER is cleared. Remaining (consuming) work, NOT blocked: the honk-tutorial screenshots live on metis -> pull to this box -> upload via /cdn -> embed the CDN URLs in TUTORIAL.md (replacing local/relative paths) -> Zig go-ahead to submit. Leaving open for that image-hosting + embed step and the Zig review gate. (Supersedes the earlier "BLOCKED on one-time setup" note above.)

title: cadence: monthly contribution-floor guard (never miss a month)
description: Recurring guard. The program floor is >=1 public, project-tied contribution per month, and monthly leaderboards are NOT recalculated for late entries. Each month: confirm an anchor submission is shipped+submitted in the first half of the month. If unmet mid-month, pull the highest-ready submission/idea bead. Track months-with-contribution in SUBMISSIONS.md. Ref: refs/program/strategy-top-ambassador.md (cadence engine).

title: submission: first general-purpose skill in aaif/public-agents (orchestrator-worktree)
description: FORMAT: project contribution (SKILL.md PR) + explainer video. PROJECT: public-agents (+ AGENTS.md). LEVERAGE (highest): public-agents is ~6wks old, 4 stars, only 2 AAIF-internal skills; recipes/ and prompts/ dirs don't exist yet; ZERO general-purpose engineering skills. It uses the Agent Skills SKILL.md format — Andrew's exact format. Land the first non-internal skill = inaugural artifact in the LF foundation's library, installable across goose/Claude Code/Codex/Gemini. Copy-adapt his orchestrator/worktree-dispatch pattern. SOURCE: refs/projects/agents-md-and-public-agents.md (#3). ACCEPTANCE: merged PR to aaif/public-agents with a working SKILL.md + README conventions; installs via npx skills add; genuinely useful to others. Points 5-50.

title: submission: 'The CLAUDE.md files that run a robot fleet' — annotated real configs
description: FORMAT: blog + companion public repo (annotated examples). PROJECT: AGENTS.md. LEVERAGE: Andrew's single strongest asset — a real, battle-tested CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md corpus across a 4-umbrella harness. Almost nobody shares production-grade multi-agent configs with the scar-tissue reasoning. Strong Dev Interrupted/LinkedIn carry. SOURCE: refs/projects/agents-md-and-public-agents.md (#1). ACCEPTANCE: published blog + repo, line-by-line 'why each rule earns its place', vendor-neutral, /zig-voice voice, /scrutinize'd. Points 15.

title: submission: AGENTS.md pattern + anti-pattern catalog (answers issue #207)
description: FORMAT: tutorial/long-form reference (blog + PR to agents.md docs). PROJECT: AGENTS.md. LEVERAGE: open issue agentsmd/agents.md#207 literally requests enterprise patterns + anti-patterns; no canonical pattern doc exists for a 60k-repo standard → evergreen, SEO-durable. Ground in GitHub's 2,500-file findings + his harness (three-tier boundaries, command-first, nearest-file scoping, delegate-don't-describe). SOURCE: refs/projects/agents-md-and-public-agents.md (#2). ACCEPTANCE: published catalog + a docs PR; each pattern grounded in a config he runs. Points 20.

title: submission: 'Driving a 30B local coder with goose on a Mac Studio' — the tool-calling reality check
description: FORMAT: tutorial + screen-recorded video. PROJECT: goose (+ MCP). LEVERAGE: serves goose's #1 roadmap theme (open/local models); the Ollama 4096-context trap + which local models survive goose's tool-call demands is chronically under-documented; almost nobody can write it authentically. Uses pico + Ollama + qwen3-coder-30b. SOURCE: refs/projects/goose.md (#2). ACCEPTANCE: reproducible tutorial + video, real hardware, verified config; honest about what breaks. Points 20+15.

title: submission: 'Observability for an agent fleet: OTel traces to a glanceable status lexicon' (talk/video) + join wg-observability
description: FORMAT: talk or video, dual-purpose with wg-observability-and-traceability deliverable. PROJECT: agentgateway (+ WG). LEVERAGE: agentgateway OTel tracing as the data source; his tmux-lexicon/pulse/beads as the human-facing layer. The early-stage observability WG lacks exactly this practitioner UX perspective — leadership-tier path. SOURCE: refs/projects/agentgateway-and-working-groups.md (#3). ACCEPTANCE: a published talk/video + a concrete WG contribution; confirm WG access via support@aaif.io. Points 25-30.

title: skills: /gateway-watch — near-real-time gateway monitoring (thresholds/anomalies)
description:
New /gateway-watch skill — near-real-time fleet monitoring.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Two mechanisms: Monitor log-tail (failure-signature coverage) + periodic incremental DuckDB query (30s+ interval).
- Watches error-rate/latency/token-blowout/429/new-identity; one alert line per breach; clean tick returns nothing.
- Breach queries VERIFIED against a live snapshot; read-only; snapshot-cost caveat documented; privacy-clean.

title: elevate: AAIF §4 drop backlog: public-agents 'document-the-system' convention + agent-affordances decision guide (sharpens aaif-49y)
description:
Two concrete AAIF monthly-drop candidates fall out of §4, each sharpening the open aaif-49y pipeline beyond 'azigler/skills matches vercel-labs/skills'. (a) DOCUMENT-THE-SYSTEM CONVENTION (ship-the-system): a public-agents README convention that an entry documents all five system layers (triggered / isolated / contextualized / made visible / governed), with azigler/skills as the worked example — framed STRICTLY as a showcase convention, NOT an AGENTS.md schema change; Eve's instructions.md-vs-AGENTS.md divergence is the supporting convention-map. (b) AGENT-FACING-AFFORDANCES DECISION GUIDE (sellerai): a PRESCRIPTIVE guide mapping the conflated inbound-affordance family — AGENTS.md vs llms.txt vs an MCP server vs ACP-style transactional affordances — reach-for-X-when-Y + worked examples + anti-patterns; seeds an 'agent-readiness' audit dimension for /redline (LinearB content). Alternate (a) and (b) across months; each must ship a REAL artifact (worked example / PR), not a survey, to clear the no-slop gate.

**Harness move:** Queue both as AAIF monthly drops feeding aaif-49y; pick one to ship first (the document-the-system convention is the stronger opener — it carries the ship-the-system thesis into the open lane). Draw the affordances guide from the compendium corpus already in hand (sellerai/eve/ship-the-system + MCP) so it's low-marginal-cost.
**Effort:** weekend  |  **Risk:** The no-slop gate: a 'landscape survey' or '3 conventions' comparison reads as filler — both must be prescriptive / land a real mapping or upstream PR. Don't ship both in one month; alternate.
**Connects:** AAIF (monthly AGENTS.md/public-agents lane); sharpens aaif-49y; /redline + LinearB content (the agent-readiness spin-off)

📌 candidate Vibes card: AAIF: document-the-system public-agents convention + agent-affordances decision guide

Source: /elevate backfill chunk — §4 control-plane cluster, 2026-06-29 (fresh max-effort agents + cross-cluster synthesis). FINDINGS write-back in ship-the-system/FINDINGS.md.

title: radar: 2026-W27 AAIF landscape learnings (baseline)
description:
BASELINE (first /aaif-radar run, 2026-07-03). 47 approved scorecards to date; issues to #117.

TYPE MIX (over-rotation): blog_post ~53% dominates the field — the lowest-leverage written type (15 pts). tutorial ~19%. The high-value types are near-empty: conference_talk and short_technical_video 1 each; workshop/course 0 ever.

PROJECT MIX: MCP ~66% (mostly pre-7-28 explainers); agentgateway is the thinnest of the four core projects (~17%).

UNDER-SERVED LANES (our opportunity, framed by developer value):
- tutorial x agentgateway — a high-value type on the thinnest project (the gist walkthrough, 18o.28).
- conference_talk — the single highest-value type (30), almost absent in the field.
- deep empirical/methodological content — the field is explainers; verified-against-real-data is open.
- goose local-inference + MCP 7-28 stateless — explicitly campaign-wanted, under-covered.

GRADING CALIBRATION: the documented detected_type->base_points model HOLDS with ZERO drift (tutorial 20, blog 15, talk 30, social 5, video 15, meetup 25, project_contribution 5/15/25). The only total!=base adjustment across 47 remains the known weak-project-tie meetup dock. /aaif-review predictions stay accurate — no model update needed.

DELTA 2026-07-04 (same-week re-run): +3 submissions (#118-120), 0 new scorecards (still 47) -> grading model still holds, zero drift. Two trend signals: (1) AGENTS.md work is maturing from explainers toward TOOLING + hands-on build-alongs (a linter/scaffolder and a build-along landed) — the explainer window there is closing; marginal value is now tooling + verifiable hands-on. (2) agentgateway GOVERNANCE content is beginning to appear (a virtual-keys / per-user-token-budget piece) — the thinnest core project starting to fill on the governance side. Opportunity read: the agentgateway-governance lane is warming, not empty (timely for the honk tutorial 18o.28, still differentiated as a full o11y/governance-plane TUTORIAL vs a single-feature blog); AGENTS.md's tooling shift favors the tooling-shaped ideas (18o.7 pattern catalog, 18o.13 converter) over another explainer.

Participant-free by design (no-gossip rule). Full private detail + week-over-week state: .local/radar/2026-W27.md.

title: idea: 'Zero-trust agentgateway with Tailscale identity' — gate MCP/LLM traffic by tailnet identity (tailscaled whois: node + email), off-mesh requests 403, no new IdP. Uniquely in Zig's lane (existing mesh). Implement ourselves first (see explore-umbrella bead), then write the tutorial + submit. Predicted type: tutorial (20 pts). Parent epic aaif-ambassador-program-18o.
notes: Implementation exploration lives at umbrella bead explore-9qj3 (~/explore). Flow: explore/implement the tailscale-auth pattern ourselves -> write the tutorial here -> submit (gated on Zig).

title: skills: retrofit gateway-audit to the DuckDB request-log source (Source B)
description:
Add the request-log DB (DuckDB) as gateway-audit's preferred source, keeping the text-log grep recipe as fallback.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Source A = request-log DB via DuckDB (PREFERRED when config.database.url set); Source B = text-log grep (fallback).
- One composite audit query yields the whole report in a single pass; VERIFIED against a live 259-row snapshot.
- Cross-refs the cookbook + the three sibling skills; privacy-clean (no keys/IPs/payloads).

title: upskill for AAIF: ship azigler/skills with re-runnable 'upskill eval' receipts (extends aaif-49y)
description:
From upskill/FINDINGS.md opportunity 2. aaif-49y (AAIF monthly-drop pipeline; first drop = azigler/skills) names the differentiator as 'attach /ab eval RECEIPTS — the thing no skills publisher ships.' But /ab structurally CANNOT be a public receipt — it runs on Zig's Claude SUBSCRIPTION via subagent dispatch, so a stranger cloning azigler/skills can't reproduce it. upskill CAN: Apache-2.0, 'pip install upskill', vendor-neutral (~699 stars) — a third party runs 'upskill eval skills/<name>' and watches the same skill-lift + token-savings land. So upskill EXTENDS /ab into the PUBLIC lane (not duplicate): /ab stays the private taste-gated internal verifier; upskill is the citable, outsider-reproducible proof the drop ships WITH the skills. Move: first azigler/skills drop ships per skill an evals/ dir + a committed 'upskill eval' results card (lift + token-savings + the test cases + provenance); README convention = 'the only agent-skill repo where every skill ships a reproducible pass-rate — re-run it yourself with upskill eval.' Pick 2-3 MECHANICALLY-checkable skills first (/commit gitmoji+trailer, proof-token wording) — never editorial ones (/zig-voice). Clears the no-slop gate by construction (a novel primitive + a real re-runnable measurement). RISK: upskill's default tests are SYNTHETIC — publishing '100% pass' on a self-written test is teaching-to-your-own-exam, WORSE than no receipt; the published receipt MUST run on a real/curated corpus and DISCLOSE provenance; never ship a synthetic-only green. Active arc: AAIF. Source: upskill/FINDINGS.md

title: setup: DuckDB request-log cookbook (refs/) — shared foundation for the gateway skills bundle
skills: /gateway-cost — token + cost accounting via DuckDB (tokens-only honesty)
description:
New /gateway-cost skill — token + cost accounting from the request-log DB via DuckDB.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Token accounting: totals + per-identity + per-model + per-day, NULL-safe; cache economics (cache_read vs cache_creation).
- Tokens-only honesty: show $ only when count(cost)>0; NULL cost -> tokens-only + name a cost catalog as the fix.
- Every query VERIFIED against a live snapshot (cache_read 9.36M, rows_priced 0); privacy-clean, no payloads.

title: decision: the idea->explore->aaif-content pipeline (explore=research/elevation; aaif=spec+content)
notes:
The repeatable process this arc establishes (Zig, 2026-07-01):
1. IDEA + EXPLORE + RESEARCH + ELEVATE -> ~/explore/<topic>/ (beads prefix explore, refs/, FINDINGS.md, DESIGN.md, /elevate). Exploration, verification, research live here.
2. SPEC + CONTENT -> aaif (beads prefix aaif): /spec the piece -> /check -> build -> /aaif-review self-review -> Zig manual check -> publish -> (Zig go-ahead) submit.
3. AAIF beads reference explore bead IDs by hand (cross-repo; br deps do not span repos).
First instance = the agentgateway arc: explore-9qj3 + explore-if4y (research/build) feed aaif-5ad (skills) + aaif-715 (article/submission).

title: OKF + the verification layer it lacks: publish the compendium OKF bundle WITH a conformance/freshness proof-gate (AAIF drop; extends aaif-49y)
description:
From okf/FINDINGS.md opp 3. OKF is agent-first but ships ZERO verification — it has a timestamp field + a reserved log.md, but NOTHING checks that a concept doc is conformant, true, or current ('just files, no runtime'). That is exactly the gap aaif-49y's generalized proof-gate primitive (ledger done-line + machine-verifiable token + a hook that RE-RUNS the proof) fills. So OKF hands po4 a timely marquee VEHICLE: publish the compendium OKF bundle (from the emit-shim bead) WITH a conformance/staleness gate + a committed receipt — the one thing no other early OKF adopter will ship. Vendor-neutral, squarely in the AGENTS.md/public-agents lane AAIF stewards, clears the no-slop gate (a novel primitive + a real measurement, not a repackage of azigler/skills). Move (as an AAIF monthly drop): (1) the OKF bundle, (2) a small okf-verify that checks required-type conformance + flags docs stale against their git timestamp, (3) a committed receipt. COMPLEMENTS, does not displace, po4's proof-gate flagship — same primitive aimed at OKF. Ship the conformance+freshness gate first (the fuller /ab-receipt version is gated on explore-ldfm). Present as 'OKF + the missing check' (an EXTENSION), never 'OKF requires this.' Active arc: AAIF. Source: okf/FINDINGS.md

title: skills: /gateway-trace — single-request/session deep-dive (id-keyed + payload join)
description:
New /gateway-trace skill — single-request/session deep-dive.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Single-request timeline keyed on id (NOT trace_id — NULL without OTLP, verified 0/259); session = honest time-slice heuristic.
- Payload join behind a loud privacy gate (structure only: json_valid/bytes/message-count — never content).
- Queries VERIFIED against a live snapshot (metadata only); src.addr masked as <caller-ip>; privacy-clean.

title: spec: rewrite 'If you give a Goose an MCP server' — substance-first re-architecture + skills 5->1 + workflow hardening
description:
GOAL: Re-architect the AAIF tutorial "If you give a Goose an MCP server" into a genuinely useful, substantive, technical-leader-respecting agentgateway tutorial; consolidate the 5 gateway-* skills into ONE agentgateway skill FIRST; then harden the draft via a subagent workflow; then ship to the review gist (after Zig's okay), gist FIRST then metis. Zig-directed after he rejected the v5 "nursery-rhyme" draft as over-whimsical, vaporous, and structurally gapped. Execute end-to-end in a FRESH session (this one is context-saturated); onboard reads this bead + .local/goose-tutorial-execution.md + the handoff note.

WHY (v5 gaps to fix):
1. NO model-setup step. The tutorial routes the local model through the gateway (the whole o11y point) yet never configures it. The real first step is configuring the model.
2. The MCP step never explains the actual move. It must teach: federate raw MCP servers as targets -> the gateway VIRTUALIZES them into ONE endpoint -> scope that endpoint per-key with CEL so each agent/session discovers only its allowed tools. That per-agent scoping is the core value and is currently absent.
3. Too much AI/whimsy language ("Here's the half most local setups skip"...); vaporous; not technical enough; would not earn a technical leader's respect. Dial whimsy WAY back.
4. Skills section buries real work. 5 is bloat; "watch" is misleading (a skill can't watch continuously); no examples of what each is or how they combine. -> ONE agentgateway skill with components + real usage examples.
5. Step 5 "parts that will trip you" -> CUT entirely.
6. "Why a gateway and not just a smaller server" -> false comparison (a gateway IS a server); bad header; useless copy -> CUT.
7. The "If You Give a Mouse a Cookie" conceit is MISUSED in places. Recognizable children's story; use it correctly. The FORMAT is good -> use it for the SECTION HEADERS / structure (the "zoom out" moves), not scattered cutesy lines.

SUBSTANCE TO TEACH (verified vs the live config + agentgateway docs; generic shapes here, private IPs/paths in .local):
- MODEL SETUP: agentgateway `llm` block = a `provider` (e.g. `provider: ollama`, `params.baseUrl: http://localhost:11434/v1`) + a `models:` list, each `provider: {reference: <name>}`. Register your local model -> model calls route through the gateway's LLM listener, so model calls AND tool calls land in the SAME request log. Be opinionated: "I'm using Ollama + qwen3:8b; use whatever you like." Fold the context-window reality here: small models default to a tight window (~4096 for qwen3:8b); raise with `num_ctx` / env `OLLAMA_CONTEXT_LENGTH` (~32768) or tool schemas overrun the context and the model silently stops calling tools.
- MCP VIRTUALIZATION + SCOPING (the spine): add each raw MCP server as a `target` (name + stdio cmd) under the `mcp` block = the federated pile. The gateway presents them as ONE virtual MCP endpoint (multiplexing). `mcpAuthorization` CEL rules scope per key: `apiKey.name == "goose" && mcp.tool.name in [read_text_file, write_file, edit_file, list_directory, search_files, fetch]` grants exactly six; a broader key (cc) sees all. Unauthorized tools are FILTERED from tools/list (the client never discovers them). Give each agent/session its own key -> per-agent/per-session tool scoping off one virtual server. tiny-tools = the goose key's 6-tool view. Docs: https://agentgateway.dev/docs/standalone/latest/tutorials/mcp-federation/ | https://agentgateway.dev/docs/standalone/main/mcp/mcp-authz/ | https://agentgateway.dev/docs/kubernetes/latest/mcp/virtual/
- FACTS TO PRESERVE (fact-checked, do not re-break): filesystem server = 14 tools, NO delete tool; write_file overwrites (the destructive one, KEPT because the task writes files); move_file "fails if destination exists" (does NOT overwrite; CUT for task-need not danger); fetch = 1 tool; federated = 15; keep 6; move_file probe -> status=400 "Unknown tool: filesystem_move_file"; access-log (tool calls) vs request-log DB (model calls) split; agentgateway self-describes as "connectivity solution / proxy," NOT "data plane" (don't overclaim). Full list + config shapes: .local/goose-tutorial-execution.md.

RE-ARCHITECTURE (substance-first; longer is fine; doc links where they help; zero cutesy filler; title kept as the hook; nursery-rhyme = correct light touch in the SECTION HEADERS/structure only):
  1. Put your model behind the gateway (provider + model; opinionated Ollama+qwen3:8b; num_ctx/context window here). NEW; needs a Models-view screenshot (redact the ollama IP -> localhost).
  2. Federate your MCP servers into one virtual endpoint (targets -> one endpoint).
  3. Scope the endpoint per agent (per-key CEL; unauthorized tools vanish; the CORE value; tiny-tools = goose's cut).
  4. Watch it all in one place (model + tool calls in one log — the payoff of routing both).
  5. Prove the scope holds (move_file -> "Unknown tool" probe).
  6. One agentgateway skill (audit/cost/trace/harden as components + the request-log cookbook reference; real examples of running them as a loop; DROP "watch").
  + Team-scale closer (per-agent scoping -> role-scoped keys). CUT the gotchas step + the "why a gateway vs a server" section.

PHASE 0 — SKILLS CONSOLIDATION (do FIRST): merge aaif/.claude/skills/gateway-{audit,cost,watch,trace,harden} into ONE `agentgateway` skill. Keep audit/cost/trace/harden as documented components; DROP "watch"; the request-log cookbook (refs/gateway-request-log-cookbook.md) is the shared reference. Include real loop examples (audit the morning after -> trace a surprising call -> cost a heavy run -> harden proposes a tighter allowlist for review; read-only, human-gated, harden never applies). Update CLAUDE.md routing + file layout to one skill; update the tutorial's skill section to link it.

PHASE 1 — REWRITE (lead, grounded): produce the restructured, de-whimsied, substantive draft. Capture the new Model-setup screenshot(s) from the live gateway UI (rig + redaction in .local). Reuse existing UI screenshots (servers, tools, tool-call) where they fit.

PHASE 2 — WORKFLOW HARDENING (use the Workflow tool; Zig-directed): parallel fresh-eyes critiques on the Phase-1 draft, each a distinct lens: (a) technical-leader-respect (substantive + accurate? would a staff/principal engineer respect it?); (b) GPT-ism/AI-language audit (list every tell; zig-voice banned list); (c) helpfulness (can a reader DO this end to end? what's under-explained/missing?); (d) voice x2 — one pass WITH /zig-voice, one WITHOUT; (e) STORY FIDELITY — a dedicated agent reading the piece against "If You Give a Mouse a Cookie" to verify the conceit is used CORRECTLY (not misused) and to propose using the format for the section headers/structure. Then a SYNTHESIS agent merges into a hardened rewrite; then a FINAL COLD READ (fresh agent, no prior context). Verify EVERY technical claim survives; no GPT-isms; story correct.

DELIVERY: land in the gist once ready. Order: Zig reviews -> okay -> push to the GIST FIRST -> THEN the metis Obsidian vault (blog). Paths/frontmatter in .local. Gist must render all images.

ACCEPTANCE:
[ ] Model-setup section: present, accurate, redacted Models-view screenshot.
[ ] MCP virtualization + per-agent (per-key CEL) scoping = the clearly-explained core value.
[ ] Whimsy = correct light nursery-rhyme touch in headers/structure; body substantive + technical; opinionated stack; doc links where they help; zero cutesy filler.
[ ] 5 gateway-* skills -> ONE agentgateway skill (components + examples; no "watch"); CLAUDE.md + tutorial updated.
[ ] gotchas step + "why a gateway vs a server" CUT.
[ ] Story-fidelity verified (mouse-cookie used correctly; format in section headers).
[ ] Workflow hardening run (parallel critiques -> synthesis -> cold read); all facts verified; no GPT-isms.
[ ] After Zig's okay: pushed to the gist first, then metis; gist renders all images.
